course.title: MIT 6.S191: Introduction to Deep Learning
course.note: MIT's free open course; Lecture 1 lays out what deep learning is and the foundations of neural networks.

book.title: Dive into Deep Learning, Chapter 1: Introduction
book.note: Frames every ML task in terms of data, model, objective (loss), and optimization, exactly the framing this week teaches.

video.title: 3Blue1Brown: But what is a neural network?
video.note: A visual, intuition-first introduction to neural networks using the MNIST digit example.

blog.title: Colah's blog: Calculus on Computational Graphs (Backpropagation)
blog.note: Shows how a model plus loss becomes a computational graph whose derivatives are computed efficiently.

course.title: Stanford CS231n: Python, NumPy and Array/Tensor Tutorial
course.note: Canonical Stanford notes on arrays, shapes, array math, and broadcasting.

book.title: Dive into Deep Learning, 2.1 Data Manipulation
book.note: Hands-on PyTorch tensor creation, reshaping, indexing, broadcasting, and in-place ops.

video.title: Andrej Karpathy: Neural Networks, Zero to Hero
video.note: From-scratch lectures that build tensor and autograd objects in code with a PyTorch-like API.

blog.title: PyTorch Official Tutorial: Tensors
blog.note: The official reference on tensor initialization, attributes (shape, dtype, device), and operations.

course.title: Karpathy: The spelled-out intro to neural networks and backpropagation (micrograd)
course.note: Builds an MLP and a tiny autograd engine from scratch, deriving the forward pass and backprop step by step.

book.title: Dive into Deep Learning, 5.1 Multilayer Perceptrons
book.note: Introduces hidden layers and nonlinear activations and why MLPs go beyond linear models.

video.title: 3Blue1Brown: Backpropagation, intuitively
video.note: A visual walkthrough of how gradients flow backward through a network to update weights.

blog.title: PyTorch Official Tutorial: A Gentle Introduction to torch.autograd
blog.note: Connects backprop theory to PyTorch autograd: the dynamic graph, requires_grad, and .backward().

course.title: fast.ai Practical Deep Learning, Lesson 1: Getting Started
course.note: Builds and trains an image model end-to-end with an automatic train/validation split.

book.title: Dive into Deep Learning, 4.2 The Image Classification Dataset
book.note: Loads Fashion-MNIST and reads minibatches via a DataLoader, demonstrating batch_size and shuffling.

video.title: PyTorch Tutorial: Dataset and DataLoader, Batch Training (Patrick Loeber)
video.note: Focused walkthrough of writing a custom Dataset and wrapping it in a DataLoader.

blog.title: PyTorch Official Tutorial: Datasets & DataLoaders
blog.note: The canonical tutorial on the Dataset/DataLoader primitives, batching, and shuffling.

course.title: Stanford CS231n: Linear Classification (Softmax and SVM)
course.note: Derives softmax/cross-entropy and hinge losses with probabilistic and information-theoretic views.

book.title: Understanding Deep Learning (Simon Prince), Chapter 5: Loss functions
book.note: Derives MSE, cross-entropy, and BCE from maximum likelihood (free PDF).

video.title: StatQuest: Neural Networks Part 6, Cross Entropy
video.note: A clear, intuitive explanation of cross-entropy as the classification loss.

blog.title: scikit-learn User Guide: Metrics and scoring
blog.note: Authoritative reference for accuracy, precision/recall, F1, ROC AUC, MSE, and R2.

course.title: Stanford CS231n: Neural Networks Part 3 (Optimization)
course.note: Covers SGD, momentum/Nesterov, Adagrad/RMSProp/Adam, and learning-rate annealing.

book.title: Dive into Deep Learning, 12.4 Stochastic Gradient Descent
book.note: SGD, dynamic learning-rate schedules, and convergence behavior.

video.title: 3Blue1Brown: Gradient descent, how neural networks learn
video.note: Visual, intuition-first explanation of gradient descent and the negative-gradient update.

blog.title: Sebastian Ruder: An overview of gradient descent optimization algorithms
blog.note: The authoritative comparison of SGD variants, momentum, Adagrad/RMSProp/Adam, with practical guidance.

course.title: Stanford CS231n: Neural Networks Part 2 (Regularization)
course.note: Dedicated sections on L2/L1 regularization and dropout, plus train/val/test discipline.

book.title: Understanding Deep Learning (Simon Prince), Chapter 9: Regularization
book.note: Covers overfitting, weight decay, dropout, early stopping, and augmentation as one toolkit (free PDF).

video.title: StatQuest: Regularization Part 1, Ridge (L2) Regression
video.note: Intuitive introduction to L2/weight-decay regularization and the bias-variance trade-off.

blog.title: Dive into Deep Learning, 5.6 Dropout
blog.note: A runnable tutorial explaining dropout, with both a from-scratch and an nn.Dropout version.

course.title: Stanford CS231n: Deep Learning for Computer Vision
course.note: The canonical CNN course, from convolution and pooling through full image-classifier architectures.

book.title: Dive into Deep Learning, Chapter 7: Convolutional Neural Networks
book.note: Free PyTorch chapter on convolutions, pooling, receptive fields, and building LeNet.

video.title: CS231n Lecture 5: Convolutional Neural Networks
video.note: Focused lecture introducing convolution, filters/feature maps, and pooling.

blog.title: CS231n Notes: Convolutional Networks
blog.note: Authoritative notes on conv layers, stride/padding, pooling, and activation maps with formulas.

course.title: Stanford CS231n: Deep Learning for Computer Vision (Architectures)
course.note: The CNN course whose architecture module surveys AlexNet, VGG, GoogLeNet, and ResNet.

book.title: Dive into Deep Learning, Chapter 8: Modern Convolutional Neural Networks
book.note: Dedicated sections on batch normalization and residual networks plus other modern architectures.

video.title: CS231n Lecture 9: CNN Architectures
video.note: Canonical lecture on ResNet and the residual-connection idea that lets very deep nets train.

blog.title: Dive into Deep Learning, 8.6 Residual Networks (ResNet)
blog.note: A self-contained explainer of residual blocks with a ResNet-18 implementation in PyTorch.

course.title: Stanford CS224n: Natural Language Processing with Deep Learning
course.note: Its Language Models and RNNs lecture covers recurrence and the vanishing-gradient problem.

book.title: Dive into Deep Learning, 9.7 Backpropagation Through Time
book.note: Derives BPTT by unrolling the RNN graph and shows how vanishing/exploding gradients arise.

video.title: MIT 6.S191: Recurrent Neural Networks
video.note: Accessible lecture introducing sequence data, the RNN cell, recurrence, and BPTT.

blog.title: Andrej Karpathy: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of Recurrent Neural Networks
blog.note: Classic post building intuition for what RNNs learn via character-level language models.

course.title: Dive into Deep Learning, Chapter 10: Modern Recurrent Neural Networks
course.note: Full sections on LSTM, GRU, and sequence-to-sequence machine translation.

book.title: Dive into Deep Learning, 10.7 Sequence-to-Sequence Learning
book.note: Builds an encoder-decoder seq2seq model with teacher forcing and masked loss.

video.title: StatQuest: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Clearly Explained
video.note: Step-by-step visual explanation of LSTM gates and the cell state.

blog.title: Christopher Olah: Understanding LSTM Networks
blog.note: The definitive illustrated walkthrough of LSTM gates, with a GRU variant section.

course.title: Stanford CS231n Lecture: Self-Supervised Learning
course.note: Stanford lecture on pretext tasks, contrastive learning, and self-supervised representations.

book.title: Goodfellow, Bengio & Courville, Deep Learning, Chapter 14: Autoencoders
book.note: The standard free chapter on autoencoders and their role in learning latent representations.

video.title: SimCLR: Contrastive Learning of Visual Representations (illustrated)
video.note: A focused walkthrough of SimCLR: augmentation views, the contrastive loss, and the projection head.

blog.title: Lil'Log: Contrastive Representation Learning
blog.note: A deeply-cited survey of contrastive and self-supervised methods (SimCLR, MoCo, BYOL, SwAV).

course.title: Stanford CS231n Notes: Transfer Learning and Fine-tuning
course.note: Lays out the two core strategies (fixed feature extractor vs fine-tuning) and when to use each.

book.title: Dive into Deep Learning, 14.2 Fine-Tuning
book.note: A runnable PyTorch walkthrough of fine-tuning a pretrained ResNet-18, including layer-wise rates.

video.title: fast.ai Practical Deep Learning, Lesson 1
video.note: Trains a real image classifier end-to-end via transfer learning on a pretrained model.

blog.title: PyTorch: Transfer Learning for Computer Vision Tutorial
blog.note: Official tutorial demonstrating both fine-tuning and fixed-feature-extractor transfer learning.
